fuzzy_prd:
Hey team, we need to wrap up the autocomplete search widget thing we've been building. The basic idea is that there's a read-only field on the form, and when users tap it, a search overlay pops up so they can type and pick a value. We need this to work for both single-pick and multi-pick scenarios.

A few things came up from user feedback recently: people are saying the overlay sometimes stays open when it shouldn't, or doesn't open at all when they tap the field — we need to make sure the open/close logic is solid and that we also support a mode where the parent screen controls the overlay manually (like we did with that modal pattern from the login flow).

Also, the search results sometimes feel laggy or jumpy — we need the search wiring to handle both instant local data and async remote calls cleanly, including when the remote call fails.

For display, selected values should show up correctly in the closed field — including when the data is a nested object, which has been a pain point. And when someone clears their selection, the field should go blank immediately.

Finally, teams using this in different screens want to customize labels, button text, CSS state classes, and inject their own template content into the overlay. Make sure cleanup works too — no ghost overlays hanging around after the component is torn down.

oracle_data.golden_api_signature: ionAutocomplete(config: { placeholder?: string, cancelLabel?: string, modelToItemMethod?: Function, itemValueKey?: string, componentId?: string, searchProvider?: Function, openOnClick?: boolean, openClass?: string, closedClass?: string, templateUrl?: string, templateData?: any, multipleSelect?: boolean, selectedItemsLabel?: string, selectItemsLabel?: string, ngModelOptions?: object })

trigger_keywords: default placeholder text in the search overlay
what text appears in the search input when nothing is configured
built-in instruction text for the search field
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: When no placeholder is configured by the caller, the search input inside the overlay must display the default text 'Click to enter a value...' as its placeholder. The outer field placeholder defaults to an empty string.

trigger_keywords: default close button label
what is the default text on the cancel or done button
built-in completion label for the overlay button
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The default label for the overlay close/cancel button is 'Done'. The button must also carry both a button class and a clear-style class. Custom text can be supplied by the caller to override this default.

trigger_keywords: CSS classes on the overlay container
default open and closed state class names
how does the overlay indicate its open or closed state visually
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The overlay container uses 'closed' as the default class when hidden and 'open' as the default class when visible. When the caller supplies custom class names for open and closed states, the default classes must not be applied at all — only the custom classes should be toggled.

trigger_keywords: disable automatic opening on field tap
prevent the overlay from opening when the field is clicked
external or manual overlay control mode
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: When automatic opening is disabled (openOnClick set to false), tapping or activating the outer field must leave the overlay in its closed state. The overlay can still be opened and closed programmatically via explicit open and close calls. This mirrors the manual modal control pattern used elsewhere in the codebase.

trigger_keywords: how search provider is called with undefined input
skip provider call when query is undefined
provider invocation rules for empty versus undefined query
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The search provider must NOT be called when the query is undefined. It MUST be called for an empty string query and for any non-empty query string. The query value is passed through to the provider unchanged. If a componentId is configured, it is included alongside the query in the provider call payload.

trigger_keywords: async provider results before promise resolves
search item list state while waiting for async data
handling promise rejection from search provider
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Before an asynchronous search provider resolves, the search item list must remain empty. After successful resolution the list is populated. If the resolved value is a response wrapper object containing a 'data' property, the items are read from that data field. If the promise rejects, the rejection must be forwarded through the original promise chain and a rejection callback must be triggered.

trigger_keywords: displaying nested object field as the selected value
itemValueKey for deep object paths
how to show a sub-property of a selected object in the closed field
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: When a field path (itemValueKey) is configured, the component must traverse the object using that path to extract the display value. This works for both direct keys and nested dot-notation paths (e.g., 'key1.key2'). The path can be set statically at configuration time or supplied dynamically. When no field path is configured and the item is an object, the whole object is returned as the projected value. Primitive values are always returned unchanged.

trigger_keywords: overlay element persists after component is torn down
cleanup when the field scope is destroyed
removing the modal from the DOM on destroy
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: When the owning field scope is destroyed, the generated overlay element must be removed from the document entirely. After destruction, querying the document for the overlay container should return zero elements.

trigger_keywords: forwarding debounce or model update options to the search input
ngModelOptions passed to the generated search field
search input timing configuration
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Any ngModelOptions (such as debounce duration) configured on the outer field must be forwarded to the generated search input inside the overlay. For example, a debounce of 1000ms on the outer field must appear as 1000ms on the search input's model update controller.

trigger_keywords: custom template in the overlay
templateUrl or templateData binding
injecting caller content into the search overlay
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The overlay supports a caller-supplied templateUrl for injecting custom HTML content. The template can be bound to the field placeholder text or to a caller-provided templateData object. Both static and dynamically bound template URLs are supported. When a template is rendered it must be visible (display block) and its bound text must reflect the configured data.

trigger_keywords: labels in multiple selection mode
selectedItemsLabel and selectItemsLabel configuration
section headings for selected and available items in multi-select
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: In multiple-selection mode, the overlay must display a caller-provided label for the section showing already-selected items and a separate caller-provided label for the section showing available search results. The available-items divider/label must be absent when no search results exist and must appear once results are added.

trigger_keywords: clearing the selected value
what happens to the outer field after the model is set to null or undefined
resetting the displayed value in the closed field
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: When the bound selection model is cleared (set to null, undefined, or empty), the outer field must immediately display an empty string. Before the clear the field shows the previously projected value; after the clear it shows nothing.

trigger_keywords: how should I organize the code
single file or multiple files
project structure
directory layout
should I split into multiple files
monolithic or modular
how complex is the codebase
what files should I create
how to structure the implementation
should I separate business logic from IO
decouple core from IO
should business logic touch stdin stdout
how to separate parsing from logic
SOLID principles
separation of concerns
adapter pattern
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Scale-driven code organization: match physical structure to domain complexity. For micro-utilities or simple scripts a single well-organized file is fine. For systems with multiple distinct responsibilities (I/O routing, business rules, formatters, etc.) you MUST use a multi-file layout (e.g. src/, tests/) — never a single god file. Do not over-engineer simple problems, but strictly avoid monolithic files for complex domains. Strict separation of concerns is mandatory: core business logic must be completely decoupled from stdin/stdout and JSON parsing. The JSON test cases are a black-box contract for the execution adapter only — NOT the internal data model. Follow SOLID: SRP (separate parsing, routing, core execution, output formatting); OCP (core engine open for extension, closed for modification); DIP (high-level modules depend on abstractions, not I/O details). The public interface must be idiomatic. Errors must be modeled properly (typed exceptions or Result/Monad patterns), not generic faults.

trigger_keywords: how does the dispatcher work
how is the program invoked
stdin stdout interface
execution adapter
how to read input
how does the test harness call my code
what is the entry point for testing
how does the runner call the program
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: You must implement an execution adapter (dispatcher) that is logically and ideally physically separate from the core domain. It reads a single JSON object from stdin, inspects the 'action' field to route to the correct core function, then prints the result to stdout matching the per-feature output contract. All policy durations in inputs are in seconds; all time points are epoch milliseconds. The adapter must not contain any business logic — every numeric result and boolean decision is delegated to the core domain.

trigger_keywords: how to write test.sh
test harness scaffold
automated test script
how should test.sh work
cases-dir parameter
where does test output go
output file naming format
how are test outputs named
rcb_tests structure
test scaffold deliverable
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Deliver a bash test harness at rcb_tests/test.sh. It must accept --cases-dir <subdir> (default: test_cases) to point at a directory of *.json case files under rcb_tests/. For each case file it runs every case in the 'cases' array against the dispatcher, writing raw stdout (no PASS/FAIL metadata) to: rcb_tests/stdout/<cases-dir>/{filename_stem}@{case_index_zero_padded_3}.txt Example: rcb_tests/stdout/public_test_cases/feature1_next_retry_at@000.txt Output is namespaced by <cases-dir> so different directories never overwrite each other. A single entry point 'bash rcb_tests/test.sh' runs the full suite with the default dir.

trigger_keywords: do you have test cases
are there public test cases
what test cases are provided
can I see the test inputs
what features have test cases
show me the test cases
what test files exist
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Yes, public test cases are provided, one file per feature. Ask me for a specific feature by name and I will give you the full test case JSON.

what does feature1 1 default field chrome expect

context_pointers.vague_reference: the modal pattern from the login flow
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The external overlay control mode implemented via the openOnClick=false configuration option, which allows the parent scope to call open/close explicitly rather than relying on field-tap events — tested in feature4_2_external_overlay_control.json

context_pointers.vague_reference: compatibility logic from that login module we did last time
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The openOnClick flag and the programmatic open/close API on the autocomplete directive, which mirrors a manual modal-control pattern and must coexist with the default tap-to-open behavior

context_pointers.vague_reference: the search results sometimes feel laggy or jumpy
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The requirement that the search item list stays empty before an async provider resolves and that ngModelOptions debounce values are forwarded to the generated search input — covered in feature3_2_async_search_provider.json and feature5_1_search_input_options.json

The itemValueKey dot-notation field path resolution used to extract display values from nested objects, and the modelToItemMethod conversion used for preloaded external model values — tested in feature2_1_initial_and_cleared_model_display.json and feature2_2_item_value_projection.json

context_pointers.vague_reference: ghost overlays hanging around after the component is torn down
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The $destroy lifecycle hook on the field scope that must remove the overlay DOM element from the document, verified by querying container count before and after destruction — tested in feature4_4_overlay_lifecycle_cleanup.json

oracle_data.fallback_response: That detail isn't something I have full context on right now — you'd need to dig into the existing implementation or check with the original dev who set up the autocomplete widget. Try searching the codebase for related config options or look at how the existing test scenarios are structured.

feature1_1_default_field_chrome.json.description: Render a single-selection autocomplete field without optional configuration and expose the default outer field, search field, icon, button, and closed overlay state.

cases.expected_output:
outer_type=text
outer_readonly=true
outer_has_widget_class=true
outer_placeholder=
search_type=search
search_has_widget_search_class=true
search_placeholder=Click to enter a value...
placeholder_icon=search
cancel_has_button_class=true
cancel_has_clear_class=true
cancel_label=Done
container_state=closed

feature1_2_custom_prompt_and_cancel_text.description: Render caller-provided prompt text on both the closed field and the overlay search field, and render caller-provided text on the overlay close button.

cases.expected_output:
outer_placeholder=placeholder value
search_placeholder=placeholder value
cancel_label=Cancel Button

feature2_1_initial_and_cleared_model_dis.description: Render the closed field from absent, preloaded, nested, dynamically addressed, and cleared selection data without requiring the user to open the overlay.

feature2_2_item_value_projection.json.description: Project item display or stored values by returning primitives unchanged, extracting direct object fields, returning whole objects when no field path is supplied, and extracting nested object fields.

feature3_1_sync_search_provider.json.description: Invoke the search provider only for defined queries, pass empty and non-empty queries through unchanged, optionally include a component identifier, and render the returned search items.

feature3_2_async_search_provider.json.description: Accept asynchronous search results, keep the search list empty until the asynchronous operation settles, read response-wrapper data payloads, and forward rejection notifications to the original promise chain.

feature4_1_overlay_open_close.json.description: Toggle the search overlay between closed and open states through normal field activation and close-button activation.

feature4_2_external_overlay_control.json.description: When automatic opening is disabled, keep the overlay closed on field activation while still allowing explicit open and close commands to control it.

feature4_3_custom_overlay_classes.json.description: Use caller-provided CSS state classes instead of the default open and closed classes when changing overlay state.

cases.expected_output:
initial_has_custom_closed=true
initial_has_custom_open=false
initial_has_default_closed=false
initial_has_default_open=false
after_click_has_custom_closed=false
after_click_has_custom_open=true
after_click_has_default_closed=false
after_click_has_default_open=false

feature4_4_overlay_lifecycle_cleanup.jso.description: Remove the generated overlay element from the document when the owning field scope is destroyed.

feature5_1_search_input_options.json.description: Forward model update options configured on the closed field to the generated search input inside the overlay.

feature5_2_custom_templates.json.description: Render a caller-supplied overlay template from a static or dynamic URL and bind template text to field prompt data or caller-provided template data.

feature6_1_multiple_selection_labels.jso.description: In multiple-selection mode, render caller-provided labels for the selected-items section and for the available-items section once search results exist.

feature6_2_multiple_selection_available_.description: In multiple-selection mode, omit the available-items divider when there are no search results and show it after results are added.

feature6_3_multiple_selection_cancel.jso.description: In multiple-selection mode, close the overlay when the close button is activated.
